creator: jmallett@xMach.org
text:
Like the man says: Close the bug.

--
[ Joseph Mallett            <jmallett@xMach.org> ] [ http://srcsys.org ]
[ xMach Core Team         xMach: Proactively Unbloated Microkernel BSD ]
[ FreeBSD, NetBSD, & xMach User; (Obj)C(++) Coder ] [ http://xMach.org ]

On Thu, 24 May 2001, Reginald S. Perry wrote:

> Short answer. Close the bug.
>
> Longer answer. After further investigation, I decided that I must have
> hoarked the motherboard when I installed the system. I then moved to a
> single AMD K6-III 450 which I used for about 6 months. I then got a deal
> on a Digital Prioris machine with four Pentium Pro 200s with 512K of cache
> on the chip which I have been running for the past couple of years as my
> BSD server (4.3 STABLE) with no problems at all.
>
> -Reggie
>
